RULING:
PLAINTIFF''S MOTION FOR ORDER (1) CONTINUING THE TRIAL DATE AND (2) RE-OPENING EXPERT DISCOVERY FOR THE LIMITED PURPOSE OF REPLACING DR. HERMAN BRUCH

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiff's motion for order (1) continuing the trial date and (2) re-opening expert discovery for the limited purpose of replacing Dr. Herman Bruch is granted. Plaintiff demonstrated good cause for a trial continuance, as required by CRC 3.1332. Pursuant to C.C.P. section 2024.050, plaintiff demonstrated that expert discovery should be re-opened. The August 31, 2015 trial date is continued to February 1, 2016 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. The parties have signed a stipulation extending the five-year statute of limitations to February 29, 2016. Expert discovery is re-opened to allow Dr. Richard Levy to examine plaintiff, prepare and produce a report, and be offered for deposition. Plaintiff's examination shall be completed by Dr. Levy on or before October 2, 2015. Dr. Levy's report shall be served on or before October 23, 2015. Dr. Levy shall be offered for and deposition commenced on or before November 13, 2015. Defendants may augment or amend their expert witness designation limited only to responding to Dr. Levy's information and testimony, if necessary. If so, defendant's new experts or initial experts who provide new information must be offered for deposition. Defendant's supplemental designations and expert reports in response to Dr. Levy shall be served on or before December 18, 2015. This limited expert discovery closes on January 28, 2016. All fact discovery and all other expert discovery remain closed. The request by opposing defendants that plaintiff bear all costs associated with Dr. Levy is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-03-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Chris Strunk of GORDON & REES LLP (415-463-8600), appearing for defendant / moving party Rosendahl Corporation
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Michael Jimenez

RULING:
DEFENDANT ROSENDAHL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rosendahl Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's insufficient questioning of plaintiff during deposition precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. However even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and the declaration of Steven Compton, Ph.D. would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (asbestos welding gloves and blankets) attributable to defendant. Defendant does not dispute that the welding gloves and blankets used by its employees contained asbestos, for purposes of this motion. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating the causation element of plaintiff's claim because it did not present evidence to demonstrate that any asbestos exposure attributable to defendant was not, in reasonable medical probability, a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related disease(s). (Rutherford v. Owens-Illinois, Inc. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 953.) Plaintiff's objections to the declaration of William Ringo, Ph.D., CIH, CSP are sustained. However, even if Dr. Ringo's declaration was admissible in its entirety it would be insufficient to make the requisite showing on this issue. The Court will not consider defendant's August 28, 2015 supplemental reply brief or the August 31, 2015 second supplemental Strunk declaration because they are untimely, violate plaintiff's due process rights and the purported deposition transcript is a rough draft. Plaintiff's objections to these documents are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
